spec_doc:
# Runtime `__HEXA_RC=` Emission Contract — Spec (Recovered)

Status: AUTHORITATIVE — recovered from source `/Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/self/main.hexa` (cmd_run + try_cache_exec). NOT observation-only.

Cycle: `runtime-hexa-rc-emission-contract-spec` (2026-04-27)
Carry-forward origin: `b-runtime-emission-spec` from `2026-04-27_kick-dispatch-rc-propagation-audit_omega_cycle.json` L142.
Policy: documentation-only; READ-ONLY against hexa-lang.

## 1. What Is `__HEXA_RC=`?

When a `.hexa` script invokes another `.hexa` script via `hexa run …` (the user-facing CLI, dispatched through `self/main.hexa::cmd_run`), the runtime wraps the actual interpreter (`build/hexa_interp` or the AOT cache slot `<HEXA_CACHE>/<hash>/exe`) inside a parent-death-watching `sh -c` wrapper. The wrapper unconditionally appends a single line `__HEXA_RC=<int>\n` to stdout immediately after the inner process exits, where `<int>` is the rc of `wait $C` for the inner process.

This sentinel is the inner exit code. It is distinct from any outer rc the *caller* may also append (e.g. `; echo __RC=$?` in nexus shell-quoted exec sites).

## 2. Verbatim Emit Construct

Two byte-for-byte identical sites in `self/main.hexa` build the wrapper command. The literal shell template:

```
exec 3<&0; ( <FFI_ENV> <VM_ENV> '<INTERP_OR_CACHED>' '<SCRIPT_OR_NONE>' <ARG_TAIL> <&3 2>'<STDERR_TMP>' ) & C=$!; ( while kill -0 $C 2>/dev/null; do [ $PPID -eq 1 ] && { kill -9 $C 2>/dev/null; exit; }; kill -0 $PPID 2>/dev/null || { kill -9 $C 2>/dev/null; exit; }; sleep 0.05; done ) & wait $C; echo __HEXA_RC=$?
```

Interp path (main.hexa:2030):
```
let cmd = "exec 3<&0; ( " + ffi_env + vm_env + "'" + interp + "' '" + script + "'" + arg_tail + " <&3 2>'" + stderr_tmp + "'" + watch + "; echo __HEXA_RC=$?"
```

AOT cache hit path (main.hexa:1486): identical structure with `cached` instead of `interp + script`.

## 3. Format

| Aspect | Value |
|---|---|
| Marker prefix | `__HEXA_RC=` (10 bytes) |
| Payload | base-10 integer rc of `wait $C` (0..255 native; 128+N for signal N) |
| Terminator | single `\n` |
| Stream | stdout (captured popen pipe) |
| Position | always the LAST stdout line |
| Cardinality | exactly one per cmd_run invocation |

Receiver: `run_stream_with_sentinel` at main.hexa:1433 — `last.starts_with(mark)` then `to_int(s.substring(len(mark), len(s)))`. Streaming buffer at main.hexa:1389 ensures only the truly-final occurrence is consumed (mid-stream coincidental emissions get flushed when next line arrives).

## 4. Outer __RC= vs Inner __HEXA_RC=

Outer __RC= is emitted by the CALLER's own shell wrapper (e.g. nexus tools append `; echo __RC=$?`); reports the rc of the outer hexa run / sh -c. Inner __HEXA_RC= is emitted by the hexa-lang runtime; reports the rc of the inner hexa_interp via wait $C. When dispatch shells through docker / ssh / pipelines, outer can be 0 while inner is non-zero. Real tail observed: `__RC=0__HEXA_RC=3` (live-spawn-smoke-with-minted-sid witness L34) — bug class fixed in 6 nexus/tool sites on 2026-04-27.

## 5. Concurrency / Cardinality

Exactly one __HEXA_RC=N per cmd_run. Watchdog subshell does NOT emit anything. wait $C blocks until inner exits, then echo is the next syntactic command — no interleaving possible.

## 6. Non-Emission Cases

| Scenario | Emitted? | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Inner clean exit | YES (rc=0) | happy path |
| Inner non-zero | YES (rc) | inner-rc parsers detect |
| Inner segfault / signal N | YES (128+N) | POSIX wait semantics |
| Watchdog kill -9 | YES (137) | indistinguishable from external SIGKILL |
| sh -c perm-denied on binary | YES (126/127) | wait on nonexistent pid |
| Caller outer shell crashes pre-wrapper | NO | caller's last_index_of returns -1 → graceful default 0 |
| popen pipe broken | UNDEFINED | absent sentinel detected |
| `hexa lsp` (exec_replace) | NO — by design | runtime.c:3274 documents the suppression |

## 7. Caller Cross-References

Nexus tool/ (7 sites, verbatim from 2026-04-27 audit witness):
1. /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool/kick_dispatch.hexa:929 — claude_code slot-rotation
2. /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool/kick_dispatch.hexa:957 — codex_cli/gemini_cli single-shot
3. /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool/kick_dispatch.hexa:568 — _auto_absorb_witness
4. /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool/atlas_absorb_hook.hexa:147 — post-Write hook
5. /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool/witness_emit.hexa:142 — _emit_one
6. /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool/atlas_absorb_sweeper.hexa:140 — sweep-mode
7. /Users/ghost/core/nexus/tool/kick_bench.hexa:471 — 3-way status ladder

Helper: `_extract_inner_hexa_rc` at kick_dispatch.hexa:100-114 (canonical reusable parser).

Hexa-lang in-tree: main.hexa:1389 (buffer), main.hexa:1433 (parser), tool/roadmap_integrate.hexa:267 (mirror).

Hive: 0 active parsers (only archived ps aux snapshots in exports/sessions/_archive_claude/*.txt).

## 8. Versioning

First commit: 3d3668b9 (2026-04-13 23:57 KST) — `fix(T35): multi-line expression continues across line-start +` (commit body landed unrelated cmd_run rt-34/35/36 hardening introducing the sentinel as part of stdin/parent-death/rc propagation triplet).

Iteration: 86004597 (2026-04-21, AOT cache mirror) → 5474f390 (2026-04-21, trailing-newline preserve) → 8d0b9421 (2026-04-23, linux parity) → 05365d2c (2026-04-25, streaming popen) → c2b6c536 (2026-04-25, eager-stream non-sentinel). Stable since.

## 9. Failure Modes for Callers

- Outer-rc-only parsing → false PASS on inner failure (FIX: two-RC ladder with _extract_inner_hexa_rc)
- Naive substring(idx+5, end).trim() → bleed-in when tail is `__RC=0__HEXA_RC=3` (FIX: first-line-only with whitespace-bounded substring)
- index_of vs last_index_of → coincidental mid-stream pickup (FIX: always last_index_of)
- Caller dies mid-popen-read → cut sentinel (FIX: treat absent as unknown, not 0, in gating)
- LSP path → no sentinel by design; do NOT add caller-side parse

## 10. Carry-Forward

1. Upstream PR to hexa-lang README.md / docs/runtime.md.
2. Future structured envelope JSON line `{"hexa_runtime_rc":N,"signal":<int|null>,"reason":"<slug>"}`.
3. Shared lib nexus/lib/hexa_rc.hexa (deferred per raw 14 wait-for-pattern).
4. Cross-repo audit (anima/seer/airgenome).
5. Atlas n6 link (atlas.append.runtime-emission-contract.n6).

## 11. Honesty Disclaimers

- Source is fully introspectable; spec is NOT observation-only.
- 7 caller refs taken verbatim from prior witness (no fabrication).
- Git blame dates verified via git show --no-patch --format.
- No hexa-lang or hive code modified.
